display._sbs_height_note: SBS panel height: 1080 (default) or 1200 for the Viture Beast's full native panel.

cameras._fps_note: OWLsight fps should match or exceed display.target_fps (90) to minimise camera-to-photon latency — sampling slower than the display wastes up to one frame interval of frame age. fps is a best-effort FrameDurationLimits request; libcamera clamps to the nearest rate the sensor supports at this resolution, so a value the sensor can't hit degrades gracefully rather than failing.
cameras._rotation_note: rotation_deg snaps to {0, 90, 180, 270}; applied as UV math in the NV12 vertex shader (no readback, ~free). Use when the CSI camera is mounted sideways/upside-down on the helmet. Settable live from Cameras > Left/Right Camera > Rotation.
cameras._csi_retry_note: A CSI sensor that comes up wedged at boot leaves an eye dark until reboot. csi_boot_retries auto-attempts a reinit that many times early at startup (also available manually via Vision > CSI Camera Controls > Reinitialize CSI Cameras, or the quick-menu Diagnostics item). csi_expected is how many CSI eyes you have (set 1 for a mono build so it never retries a second camera you don't have).
cameras._focus_note: cameras.owlsight_left/right.focus {mode: auto|manual|slave, position: 0-1000} is the saved focus profile, written automatically by the settings autosave (position sampled from the live lens, quantized to steps of 10). At boot a saved slave/manual profile is restored directly - the helmet comes back from a power cut with the last-known focus, skipping the boot AF hunt. mode 'auto' (or no key) = run the normal one-shot boot autofocus.

owlsight_left._controls_note: Per-eye AF/AE/WB/ISP/HDR controls, set from Menu > Cameras > Left/Right Camera and saved here automatically. Values match libcamera enums. af_range:0 Normal/1 Macro/2 Full · af_speed:0 Normal/1 Fast · gain:0=auto else manual analogue gain · ae_metering:0 Centre/1 Spot/2 Matrix · ae_constraint:0 Normal/1 Highlight/2 Shadows · ae_exposure_mode:0 Normal/1 Short/2 Long · flicker:0 Off/1 Auto/2 50Hz/3 60Hz · awb_mode:0 Auto/1 Incandescent/2 Tungsten/3 Fluorescent/4 Indoor/5 Daylight/6 Cloudy · brightness:-1..1 · contrast/saturation/sharpness:0..2 · noise_reduction:0 Off/1 Fast/2 HQ/3 Minimal · hdr:0 Off/2 Multi/3 Single/4 Night (IMX708 + recent libcamera). Omit the block to keep neutral defaults.

cameras._eye_source_note: Valid values: csi | usb1 | usb2 | usb3. Routes that camera feed as the fullscreen background for the given eye.
cameras._multicam_note: multicam_layout = true overrides the per-eye source pickers and renders the same quad composite in both eyes: CSI left/right (rotated per Cameras > Left/Right Camera > Rotation) side-by-side on the top half, multicam_usb_a + multicam_usb_b side-by-side on the bottom. Set the CSI rotation to 90° (clockwise) for the intended portrait fit on the top half.

lora._note: RAK4631 enumerates as ACM2 (after Teensy=ACM0, SmartKnob=ACM1). Adjust if udev assigns a different index.

wireless_controller._note: ESP32-C3 USB-serial bridge for ESP-NOW in-paw controller. Set enabled:true and adjust port once the bridge is connected.

_display_extras_note: display.fov_deg = per-eye horizontal FOV (deg), used to reproject world-pinned HUD content (HUD > Floating Window). display.timewarp_predict_ms = how far ahead (ms) the timewarp extrapolates the head pose toward display time; 12 covers typical pose age + scan-out, 0 disables prediction.

terminal._note: Floating-window terminal (HUD > Floating Window > Content: Terminal): a real shell in a pty rendered by the HUD. shell empty = $SHELL (fallback /bin/bash).

float_window._note: World-pinned floating window (HUD > Floating Window). content 0 = terminal, 1 = phone mirror; width_deg = angular width. Show/pin/keyboard focus are runtime toggles in the menu.

attitude_indicator._note: Fighter-style attitude indicator overlay (HUD > Attitude Indicator). Reads the calibrated euler of the selected IMU source, so PIT/YAW/ROL match the IMU Live Readout. full=false shows just horizon + waterline + readouts; size is the diameter as a fraction of screen height; per_eye=true draws one instrument per SBS eye half (false = single, centered - for 2D/mirror modes); text_scale multiplies the readout fonts.
attitude_indicator._smooth_note: Speed-adaptive pose filter: 0 = raw sensor, 1 = heaviest smoothing when still. Fast motion and tare jumps always track instantly.

hud._compass_smooth_note: Compass heading smoothing (HUD > Compass > Response). 0 = raw / most real-time (tracks the IMU frame-for-frame), 1 = calm. Speed-adaptive One-Euro filter: low cutoff when still, opens near-raw during a turn. The BNO086's on-chip fusion is clean, so low values stay steady.

gpio._about: Configurable GPIO switch map. Each entry in 'pins' assigns a BCM pin a short-press and optional long-press function, with pull bias ('up'|'down'|'none') and polarity (active_low=true means a momentary switch wired to GND). Edit in-HUD via GPIO > On-Board GPIO > GPIO Buttons; toggling Enabled applies instantly and 'Apply Changes Now' reloads the poller after slot edits (no relaunch needed). Functions: none, boop_snout, boop_left, boop_right, boop_both, menu_open, menu_select, menu_back, system_restart, system_shutdown, cam_af_left, cam_af_right, cam_pip_left, cam_pip_right, cam_capture_left, cam_capture_right, cam_swap, phone_ring, face_neutral, face_happy, face_angry, face_sad, face_surprised, face_return, material_rainbow, material_pride, material_progress, material_trans, material_bisexual, material_pansexual, material_lesbian, material_nonbinary, material_asexual, material_genderfluid, material_genderqueer, material_aromantic, material_intersex, cam_capture_stereo, rec_toggle, cam_zoom_in, cam_zoom_out, nv_toggle, theater_toggle, xr_recenter, face_next, face_prev, material_next, effect_next, face_bright_up, face_bright_down, face_restart, max_next, max_prev, max_clear.

coprocessor._about: OPTIONAL button/switch coprocessor (RP2350/RP2040). When enabled, an external MCU debounces the switches and streams presses to the Pi, dispatched through the same functions as the GPIO map above. Never required — leave disabled to use on-board GPIO only. Edit live in GPIO > RP2350 GPIO Expander. See docs/coprocessor-input.md.
coprocessor._variant_note: Coprocessor board, for the pin visualizer/editor only (the firmware doesn't care): 'rp2350a' = Pico 2 (GP0-29, ADC GP26-28, shown as the real 40-pin header); 'pico_plus_2' and 'pico_lipo2_xl_w' = RP2350B boards (GP0-47, ADC GP40-47, shown as a logical GP grid, with each board's reserved pins flagged); 'raw' = board-agnostic GP0-47 view. Pick it in GPIO > RP2350 GPIO Expander > Board.
coprocessor._transport_note: 'usb_serial' (USB CDC, default) or 'i2c' (specified but not implemented in v1).
coprocessor._device_note: Use a stable /dev/serial/by-id/... path matched on the coprocessor's USB serial string — NOT /dev/ttyACMn, which races with the Teensy/SmartKnob/RAK4631.
coprocessor._touch_note: TTP223 touch pads on the coprocessor's pre-assigned touch pins (indices 0-5 = GP0/GP1/GP12/GP16/GP17/GP18; see firmware config.h). A pad whose INDEX matches a boop-zone electrode (inputs.boop) fires that zone; a pad mapped here fires the named function on touch-down (both can apply). Example: { "3": "face_next", "4": "effect_next", "5": "menu_open" }.
coprocessor._replace_note: false = additive (GPIO slots + coproc both fire). true = coproc is the only button source (local GPIO poller is not started).
coprocessor._buttons_note: Maps the coprocessor's button ids to functions (same function ids as gpio.pins). The MCU reports 'button N, short/long'; the Pi decides what N means, so remapping stays here.
coprocessor._pins_note: OPTIONAL physical pin map, pushed to the firmware over PINCFG on connect so a switch's GPIO / pull / polarity / backlight is set here — no firmware reflash. Array order = button id (row 0 = id 0, matching 'buttons' above). gp = RP2350 GPIO; pull = up|down|none; active_low true = a switch to GND; led_gp = optional backlight GPIO (-1 = none). Edit visually in GPIO > RP2350 GPIO Expander > Pins (a pin visualizer + editor: pick free pins, set functions, Apply & Reload pushes it live). Omit the whole block to leave the firmware on its compiled-in config.h defaults. Firmware-fixed voice pins (I2S GP16-18, I2C GP20/21, mic GP26) and MAX7219 pins aren't runtime-remappable — avoid reusing them.

command_fifo._about: OPTIONAL local command pipe. When enabled, writing a GpioFunc id line to the FIFO drives ProtoHUD through the same functions as the GPIO map — e.g. a KDE Connect 'Run Command' doing `echo menu_open > /run/protohud/cmd`. Untrusted input: unknown ids are ignored. See docs/kdeconnect-commands.md and scripts/kdeconnect_commands.py.
command_fifo._path_note: FIFO is created (mkfifo) if absent. Use a tmpfs path (/run or /tmp). Valid command ids: menu_open, menu_select, menu_back, cam_capture_left, cam_capture_right, cam_af_left, cam_af_right, cam_pip_left, cam_pip_right, cam_swap, boop_snout, boop_left, boop_right, boop_both, phone_ring, system_restart, system_shutdown; face_neutral, face_happy, face_angry, face_sad, face_surprised, face_return (snap back to the previously-set face); material_rainbow, material_pride, material_progress, material_trans, material_bisexual, material_pansexual, material_lesbian, material_nonbinary, material_asexual, material_genderfluid, material_genderqueer, material_aromantic, material_intersex.

pip._anchor_values: top_left | top_center | top_right | bottom_left | bottom_center | bottom_right

protoface._about: Protoface LED face. mode 'daemon' (default) talks to the Python daemon over socket+shm; mode 'native' renders the face in-process in ProtoHUD (no daemon) and writes the same /dev/shm frame, so the preview and scripts/panel_driver.py work unchanged. autostart (daemon mode) launches the daemon on boot. preview is the on-glasses panel-preview placement (persisted on exit).
protoface._backend_note: protoface.backend picks the native face hardware: 'hub75' (default), 'max7219', 'rgb_matrix' (WS2812 8x8 modules), or 'led_panels' (large custom APA102/SK9822 panels on CM5 SPI - see docs/led-face-panels.md).
protoface._show_prototracer_note: Show the legacy ProtoTracer/Teensy source picker and submenu under Face Display > Base Settings. Hidden by default since the Face Display redesign; the wiring stays, so flipping this back on restores the old source switch.
protoface._expression_styles_note: protoface.expression_styles (written on save) maps an expression name to its own style {material, effect, glitch} - set from each expression's own Material / Effect / Glitch menus (Face Display > Faces and Expressions > Expressions) Every expression inherits the Default Style (Material Color / Effects / Glitch) unless its own style overrides it.
protoface._custom_expressions_note: protoface.custom_expressions (written on save) is the user-created expression list: name, base_expression (which PNG slot supplies the art), and style {material, effect, glitch}. Managed from Face Display > Faces and Expressions > Expressions (custom slots live in the same list as the built-ins); starts with 5 empty slots.
protoface._expression_triggers_note: protoface.expression_triggers (written on save) maps an expression key (built-in stem like 'happy', or 'custom_3' for custom slot 3) to its trigger recipes: hold_s (0 = latch) plus up to 3 recipes of {event: boop zone / swipe gesture / head shake / light edge, count x window_s, and WHILE-conditions: head tilt left-right beyond tilt_deg, light above-below light_lux, moving-still}. E.g. nose boop x5 = angry; left cheek while tilted left = curious. Edited under each expression's Triggers menu; the most specific matching recipe wins.
protoface._reaction_rules_note: protoface.reaction_rules (written on save) is the Reactions list (Face Display > Faces and Expressions > Reactions): named environmental reactions each with {enabled, threshold, hold_s, outcome}. Reactions: move_bright / move_dark (ambient lux edges), dizzy (accumulated fast spin, degrees), low_battery (controller battery %), loud_noise (mic level 0..1), upside_down (roll degrees). outcome = {kind: 'expression'|'gif', expression | gif_file (a filename in the gifs folder)}. The GIF picker lists every file in the folder by name with a live preview. Falling Asleep (stillness) is separate under reactions.sleepy: calm_dps is the Motion Deadzone (head motion in deg/s below it counts as 'still'), drowsy_after_s / sleep_after_s are the stillness timers, wake_dps is the motion spike that wakes the face.
protoface._effect_overlay_note: Each expression's Effect menu has an Effect Mode: Replace (the effect stands alone) or Overlay (it layers on top of the Default Style / ambient effect). Stored per expression as style.effect_overlay.

led_panels._note: Custom face panels built from APA102/SK9822 (DotStar) LEDs, up to ~2000 LEDs per panel, driven from CM5 hardware SPI at 60+ fps. Each chain = one panel on its own spidev (or chain two panels on one bus). Per-LED layout comes from map_file (JSON {"leds": [[x,y], ...]} in canvas pixels, chain order) or the cols/rows grid generator for rectangular prototypes. power_limit_a caps the frame's estimated current in software - set it to what the panel's 5V injection wiring actually supports.

protoface._fps_note: Native-mode face render rate. 60 keeps IMU-driven face motion (inertia, water slosh, particles) smooth; drop to 30 to save CPU on a loaded system.
protoface._face_colors_note: HUB75/native only. false (default) = the selected Material Color tints the face (monochrome-style). true = draw each expression's own RGB art verbatim (color faces), and open the face editor on its color canvas so faces can be drawn in color. GIFs always show their own colors; particles/background still use the material. Toggle live in Face Display > Material Color > Use Drawn Colors.
protoface._motion_particles_note: Couple directional particle effects (rain/snow/embers/steam/...) to real head motion via the IMU: precipitation leans with gravity as you tilt and gets swept sideways by quick turns. Layers that set their own direction_from keep it. Toggle live in Face Display > Effects > Motion Reactive.
protoface._face_inertia_note: The whole face slides opposite quick head motion and springs back with a small overshoot, like it has mass: eyes lag on a fast turn, bob on a nod, then settle (~0.5 s). Uses the IMU head-tracking feed. face_inertia_strength scales the maximum slide - 1.0 lets it travel up to ~10% of the panel. Toggle live in Face Display > Effects > Face Inertia.
protoface._weather_effects_note: Weather Sync: the face's ambient effect follows real conditions from the HUD weather monitor (WMO code -> rain / gentle_snow / heavy_snow / thunderstorm / clouds; a clear night shows night_sky). Applied as an override - your chosen effect returns when it clears up, and Expression Effects moods still play on top. Re-checked ~once a minute. Toggle live in Face Display > Effects > Weather Sync.
protoface._temp_effects_note: Temp Effects: ambient frost crystals when the live outdoor temperature is at/below temp_cold_c, rising heat shimmer at/above temp_hot_c. Shares the ambient slot with Weather Sync - precipitation wins, temp effects fill clear-sky gaps. Thresholds are Celsius regardless of weather.metric (display-unit temps are converted). Lives under Face Display > Effects > Temp Effects (its own submenu: Enabled + a Frost subset and a Heat subset). Frost subset: Frost Below threshold, Fractal Frost (branching fern growth + drifting snowflakes), frost_speed (creep/formation pace, 1.0 = default), Test Frost. Heat subset: Heat Above threshold, Heatwave Heartbeat (orange lub-dub glow along the frost edges), heatwave_speed (shimmer + heartbeat pace, 1.0 = default), Test Heatwave. Test Frost/Heatwave force the effect on regardless of temperature (not saved).
protoface._water_humidity_note: A 'water' particle layer can tie its fill level to the BME280 humidity sensor: set "level_from":"humidity" on the layer and relative humidity (0..100%) maps onto [level_min, level_max] (defaults 0.10..0.90) instead of the fixed "level". Toggle live via Face Display > Effects (Custom water layer) > Fill from Humidity, with Dry Level / Full Level for the range. Needs bme280.enabled.
protoface._frost_fractal_note: Frost grows in branching fractal fern patterns and large snowflakes drift in as it forms, instead of the smooth creeping sheet. Toggle: Face Display > Effects > Temp Effects > Fractal Frost.
protoface._heatwave_heartbeat_note: Heatwave adds a slow orange glow pulsing along the same panel edges the frost creeps in from, throbbing like a lub-dub heartbeat (bpm ~50). Toggle: Face Display > Effects > Temp Effects > Heatwave Heartbeat.
protoface._pride_note: pride_sharp: flags render as hard-edged distinct stripes (real-flag look) when true (default) or smooth blends when false. pride_angle: stripe rotation in degrees (90 = the usual vertical stripes, 0 = left-right, other values = diagonals). Both live under Face Display > Material Color > Pride (Sharp Bands, Rotation). Scenic gradient presets (Sunset, Ocean, ...) are mirrored about the vertical center; the Custom Gradient has its own Rotation slider and Mirror at Center toggle.
protoface._expression_effects_note: When true, the active particle effect auto-swaps to a mood preset as the face expression changes (angry->fire, happy->celebration, sad->rain, shocked->galaxy); neutral/unmapped faces restore your chosen effect. Toggle live in Face > Effects > Expression Effects.
protoface._continuous_effects_note: When true, a mirrored 2-eye face renders BOTH eyes independently (the second eye's face is flipped to keep the mirrored look) so canvas-space effects like 'water' form one continuous tank across the visor instead of one eye being a flipped copy. Costs a second face/particle render. Applied at startup.
protoface._panel_driver_note: native mode only: auto-launch scripts/panel_driver.py to drive the HUB75 panels. Set false for preview-only or if you run the driver yourself.
protoface._max7219_note: MAX7219 8x8 matrix chains. mode 'main' = MAX is the whole face (set backend:'max7219'); mode 'section' + enabled = MAX runs AS AN EXTRA SURFACE alongside the HUB75 face (teed to both). Each chain: transport 'spidev'|'gpio'|'coproc'. 'coproc' ships the SPI bytes over USB to the RP2350 button/voice coprocessor (firmware built with -DMAX_BRIDGE), which shifts them out its SPI1 (DIN=GP11, CLK=GP10) and pulses coproc_cs -> kMaxCsPins[coproc_cs] (default GP13) - so MAX runs beside HUB75 with zero CM5 GPIO. cols_chips x rows_chips is the grid (canvas_x/y = where on the render canvas it mirrors); module_positions overrides that with per-8x8-module placement for ragged/non-rectangular layouts.

max7219._content_note: section content: 'face' mirrors the face-canvas region; 'symbols' shows independent, triggerable symbols/text/patterns. Trigger via the max_next/max_prev/max_clear GpioFuncs (buttons), the Face Display > MAX7219 Layout > Content Library menu, or the command FIFO: `echo max_symbol:heart > /run/protohud/cmd`, `echo max_text:HELLO > ...`, `echo max_pattern:bars > ...`. Built-in symbols: heart star up down left right check cross smiley note excl dot; patterns: solid border checker bars blank.

protoface._native_about: native-mode options: assets_dir points at the Protoface checkout holding faces/materials/gifs (default $HOME/protohud/Protoface); panels overrides the default 2-panel mirrored layout.

hub75._about: HUB75 panel layout (native mode; drives scripts/panel_driver.py). Edit live in Face > HUB75 Layout. panel_size like '64x32'; arrangement 'horizontal'|'vertical'|'grid2x2'; panel_count 1-4.
hub75._pinout_note: HUB75 bonnet wiring: 'adafruit_bonnet' (single-connector Adafruit RGB Matrix Bonnet/HAT, default) or 'active3' (triple-connector Active-3 board). Append '_bgr' (e.g. 'adafruit_bonnet_bgr') if red/blue are swapped. Selects both the piomatter pinout and the geometry/mapper — Active-3 uses the multilane mapper, the Adafruit bonnet a plain serpentine-chained geometry.
hub75._color_order_note: Panel color-channel order: 'auto' uses the pinout's default; or force 'rgb'/'rbg'/'grb'/'gbr'/'brg'/'bgr' for oddly-wired panels (red/green swapped is usually 'grb', red/blue swapped 'bgr'). Also settable live from Face Display > HUB75 Layout > Color Order.
hub75._camera_mode_note: Camera-friendly mode (piomatter): drives the panels with extra temporal dithering / bit planes so the face reads cleanly on video (less banding/flicker). A tune-and-test knob — piomatter's PIO refresh is already stable. Toggle live in Face Display > Hardware > Camera Mode.
hub75._camera_tuning_note: Used only when camera_mode is on. camera_planes = PWM bit planes (color depth); camera_temporal_planes = temporal-dither planes. Raise camera_temporal_planes if the panel still bands on camera; lower camera_planes to trade color depth for refresh.

preview._view_values: 0 = whole face | 1 = left half | 2 = right half

scheduler._about: On-board scheduler/reminders. Networking (phone web form + Google Calendar) lives in the scheduler_daemon Python companion, which writes events.json + scheduler_status.json. ProtoHUD reads those by file poll. Paths default to ~/.local/share/protohud-scheduler/ on both sides (leave events_path/status_path unset unless you need a custom location).
scheduler._send_link_note: When true, send a tappable notification with the scheduler web-page link to the paired phone over KDE Connect once at boot (after the daemon publishes its URL and the phone is connected) — it won't auto-open. Also available on demand via Info Panel > Schedule > Send Link to Phone.

kdeconnect._about: Bridges phone notifications from KDE Connect (over LAN or USB tether) into the HUD's notification queue. Requires kdeconnectd running on the Pi and the phone paired via the KDE Connect Android/iOS app. Bridge is gated by libdbus-1-dev being available at build time; on platforms without DBus the section is silently ignored.
kdeconnect._device_id_note: Empty picks the first paired+reachable device. Set to a specific id (see kdeconnect-cli --list-devices) when multiple phones are paired.
kdeconnect._message_apps_note: CSV of appName substrings treated as chat/DMs: these get a larger toast (sender as the title, message wrapped below) that stays up ~14 s so you can read who sent it and what it says.
kdeconnect._ignore_list_note: CSV of substrings matched against a notification's title + text (case-insensitive). Anything matching is dropped — use it to mute noisy Discord servers or group chats by name. Communications > Phone (KDE Connect) > Ignore List groups the apps/senders the bridge has seen so you can mute them with one tap (muted rows show red).
kdeconnect._low_battery_note: Raise a one-shot HUD warning when the phone battery falls to/below this percent while discharging. 0 disables; it re-arms after the phone charges back above the threshold.

phone_inbox._about: Watches a directory for files dropped in from the phone (KDE Connect's Share & Receive lands them in ~/Downloads by default). New face PNGs / GIFs surface an Import toast in the HUD; accept moves the file to <watch_dir>/.processed/, dismiss moves it to <watch_dir>/.dismissed/.
phone_inbox._watch_dir_note: Empty defaults to $XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR / ~/Downloads. Point this at a dedicated dir if you want a cleaner inbox (then change KDE Connect's Share destination to match).
phone_inbox._auto_dismiss_note: 0 keeps the import toast sticky until you act on it. Setting it would let untouched files quietly skip import, which is usually not what you want.

notifications._about: The notification log (Communications > Notification Log) keeps the last 50 notifications, browseable and filterable by type/sender. persist saves it to protohud_notifications.json next to config.json so a sudden reboot doesn't lose them; toggle it live under Communications > Notification Log > Persist Log.

audio._note: Audio capture is handled by the RP2350 helmet audio processor (USB Audio UAC2). The CM5 receives pre-processed stereo and routes it to the chosen output. Run 'arecord -l' to verify the RP2350 card name after connecting via USB.

android._about: Android device mirror via scrcpy → V4L2 loopback → OpenCV.
android._prereqs: sudo apt install scrcpy v4l2loopback-dkms && sudo modprobe v4l2loopback video_nr=4 card_label=AndroidMirror exclusive_caps=1
android._anchor_values: top_left | top_center | top_right | bottom_left | bottom_center | bottom_right

apds9960._about: APDS-9960 gesture + proximity sensor on I2C 0x39, mounted near the snout for touchless input. Swipes and the proximity 'near' edge map to GPIO function ids (see gpio._about for the full list), so a gesture can do anything a button can. gesture_rotation (0/90/180/270) remaps directions for the module's mounting.

bme280._about: Bosch BME280 environment sensor on I2C (0x76, or 0x77 with SDO high): temperature, humidity and pressure, shown under System > Temperature and available to future reactions.

temperature._about: Temperature probes, read into the HUD/menu (System > Temperature) ~1 Hz and available to warn on. v1 reads DS18B20 1-Wire sensors: wire the data line to a spare GPIO (e.g. BCM 25, clear of the HUB75 bonnet) with a 4.7k pull-up to 3.3V, add `dtoverlay=w1-gpio,gpiopin=25` to /boot/firmware/config.txt, then find each probe id with `ls /sys/bus/w1/devices/` (they look like 28-XXXXXXXXXXXX). Many probes share the one data pin. id:"" uses the first probe found. type 'ds18b20' reads the CM5's 1-Wire bus; type 'coproc' reads a probe wired to the RP2350 coprocessor instead (peripheral-hub firmware; id = the 16-hex ROM from its TEMP reports). max31865/PT100 and i2c chips can be added later. A probe also drives the fan curve — point fans.temp_path at its /sys/bus/w1/devices/<id>/temperature file (or use fans.temp_probe for a coproc probe).

fans._about: Pi-driven cooling fans via software PWM on GPIO (drive a 2-pin fan through a MOSFET, or a 4-pin fan's control line). Up to 4 fans grouped into 2 independently-controlled zones. Live controls in System > Cooling Fans. Use pins HUB75 doesn't claim — see hardware/carrier-board/PINMAP.md (e.g. BCM 18/19, and 14/15 if free).
fans._min_duty_note: Fans stall below ~20% duty; this is the floor applied whenever a zone should be moving.
fans._temp_path_note: Any milli-°C sysfs file. Default = SoC temp. To ramp fans off a helmet probe instead, point this at a DS18B20 file, e.g. /sys/bus/w1/devices/28-XXXX/temperature (same milli-°C format).
fans._output_note: 'gpio' (default) = software-PWM on the CM5 lines in zones[].gpios. 'coproc' = the RP2350 coprocessor's fan pins (firmware -DPERIPHERAL_HUB, GP14/15 at 25 kHz) — the temp curve and all menu controls stay on the CM5, only resolved duties cross the USB link ('FAN <zone> <duty%>'); zones[].gpios is ignored. With 'coproc', temp_probe may name a coprocessor DS18B20 ROM id (16 hex chars, from its TEMP reports) so the curve follows a helmet probe instead of temp_path.

zones.name: Intake
zones._gpios_note: 1-2 BCM lines per zone; all fans in a zone share the zone's speed.
zones._mode_values: manual | auto (ramps speed with CPU temp between auto_min_c/auto_max_c)

mpu9250._about: HiLetgo GY-9250 / MPU-9250 backup compass via I2C. Wired to CM5 40-pin header: SDA=GPIO2(pin3), SCL=GPIO3(pin5), VCC=3.3V(pin1), GND(pin6). AD0 to GND for address 0x68.
mpu9250._mpu_addr_note: 104 = 0x68 (AD0 low). Use 105 (0x69) if AD0 tied high.
mpu9250._declination_note: Local magnetic declination in degrees. Positive = East, negative = West. Find yours at ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/calculators/magcalc.shtml
mpu9250._heading_offset_note: Mechanical mounting offset in degrees. Adjust so north reads 0 when sensor points north.
mpu9250._mag_bias_note: Hard-iron calibration offsets. Auto-updated by Calibrate Compass in the menu.

bno055._about: Adafruit BNO055 9-DOF absolute orientation sensor via I2C. On-chip sensor fusion — heading comes from the chip directly (no software AHRS needed) and stays stable through tilt. Preferred over MPU9250 when both are present (Auto IMU source picks BNO055 first). Wired to CM5 40-pin header: SDA=GPIO2(pin3), SCL=GPIO3(pin5), VCC=3.3V(pin1), GND(pin6). ADR pin floating/low = 0x28; ADR tied high = 0x29.
bno055._clock_stretch_note: The BNO055 stretches the I2C clock, which the Pi/RP1 hardware I2C handles poorly — it can read fine on an Arduino but be flaky/undetectable on the Pi. If `i2cdetect -y 1` doesn't show 28, or readings are unstable, slow the bus in /boot/firmware/config.txt: `dtparam=i2c_arm_baudrate=10000` (10 kHz). If I2C still won't work, switch to UART — set transport:'uart' (see _transport_note); this is Adafruit's recommended path on a Pi.
bno055._i2c_addr_note: 40 = 0x28 (default). Use 41 (0x29) if the ADR jumper is closed.
bno055._declination_note: Local magnetic declination in degrees. Positive = East, negative = West. Find yours at ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/calculators/magcalc.shtml
bno055._heading_offset_note: Mechanical mounting offset in degrees. Adjust so north reads 0 when sensor points north.
bno055._heading_axes_note: Axis remap for non-default mounting. 0 = factory default (X-forward, Y-left, Z-up). The chip's calibration is preserved across axis remaps so re-calibration isn't needed when this changes.
bno055._poll_hz_note: Sensor read rate. 50 Hz matches the chip's internal fusion rate; higher just resamples the same fused value.
bno055._save_calibration_note: Persist the chip's calibration profile to bno055_calib.bin (next to this file) so it survives reboots. Restored automatically at startup; saved on demand via Compass > Save IMU Calibration, and auto-saved once the chip first reaches full calibration (sys 3/3) if no file exists yet. Calibrate by rotating the head through several orientations + a figure-8 for the magnetometer until all four values read 3.
bno055._transport_note: 'i2c' (default) or 'uart'. UART sidesteps the Pi's I2C clock-stretch problem entirely (Adafruit's recommended path on a Pi). UART setup: (1) solder the PS1 jumper to 3.3V so the chip boots in UART mode (SCL/SDA become RX/TX); (2) wire BNO SDA(TX)->Pi RXD, SCL(RX)->Pi TXD, PS1->3.3V; (3) free the serial port: add 'enable_uart=1' to /boot/firmware/config.txt, disable the serial login console (raspi-config > Interface > Serial > login shell No) and 'sudo systemctl disable serial-getty@ttyAMA0'. On CM5/Pi5, prefer a dedicated RP1 UART via dtoverlay so you don't fight Bluetooth; set uart_device to that port.
bno055._uart_usb_note: Easiest UART route: a USB-UART adapter (FTDI/CP2102/CH340) instead of the Pi's hardware UART — no enable_uart, no disabling the serial console. Still set PS1->3.3V on the BNO so it boots in UART mode, then wire BNO SDA(TX)->adapter RX, SCL(RX)->adapter TX, GND, 3.3V. The adapter enumerates as /dev/ttyUSB* — point uart_device at it (see _uart_device_note).
bno055._uart_device_note: Serial port for transport:'uart'. Pi hardware UART = /dev/ttyAMA0 (or your RP1 dtoverlay port). USB-UART adapter = its /dev/ttyUSB* — but prefer the stable by-id path so it survives replug/enumeration order, e.g. /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_CP2102_USB_to_UART_Bridge_Controller_XXXX-if00-port0 (run `ls -l /dev/serial/by-id/` to find yours). A CP2102 adapter shares its USB VID:PID with the SmartKnob, so the by-id path (keyed on the adapter's serial) avoids a /dev/ttyUSBn mix-up.

bno086._about: BNO086 (CEVA BNO08x family) 9-DOF sensor over I2C, using the vendored CEVA SH-2 library (vendor/sh2). Provides a magnetometer-referenced heading with NO yaw drift, and (with head_tracking) an AR/VR-stabilized quaternion for head tracking. Set imu_source to 'bno086' (or leave 'auto' — BNO086 is preferred over BNO055 when present). Wired to CM5 40-pin: SDA=GPIO2(pin3), SCL=GPIO3(pin5), VCC=3.3V, GND, plus INT and RST to any spare GPIO.
bno086._i2c_addr_note: 74 = 0x4A (default). Use 75 (0x4B) if the SA0/ADR pin is tied high.
bno086._int_line_note: GPIO line offset (BCM number on the Pi) of the BNO086 INT/data-ready pin (active-low). Strongly recommended for I2C. -1 = no INT wired (driver polls the bus each service tick).
bno086._rst_line_note: GPIO line offset of the BNO086 RST pin (active-low). -1 = no hardware reset (the driver just waits for the hub to boot).
bno086._report_interval_note: Orientation report period in microseconds. 5000 = 200 Hz (default), 10000 = 100 Hz.
bno086._auto_calibrate_note: Keep the chip's dynamic calibration running on accel/gyro/mag and let it persist the refined calibration to its own flash automatically (plus a snapshot on clean shutdown). The mag walks itself up to s3 during normal wear - no figure-8 ritual - and it survives power cycles.
bno086._aux_interval_note: Period of the calibrated accel/gyro/mag side reports shown in GPIO > On-Board GPIO > IMU > Live Readout. 40000 = 25 Hz. The Mag row's s0-s3 is the magnetometer calibration quality the fused heading depends on.
bno086._heading_invert_note: Flip yaw->heading direction if the compass turns the wrong way for your mounting.
bno086._trim_note: Manual fine correction (degrees) added to the roll/pitch outputs - live sliders in GPIO > On-Board GPIO > IMU. For residual lean after Set Level; keep small (<15 deg), large mounting angles belong to Set Level's quaternion reorientation.
bno086._head_tracking_note: When true, the BNO086's roll/pitch/yaw also drive the imu_pose head-tracking path (timewarp + pin-in-space), taking over from the VITURE glasses' built-in IMU. Recenter/tare from Compass > Recenter Head Tracking.

video._note: Video recording. camera = left|right|both; fourcc is a 4-char OpenCV codec (mp4v widely available, avc1 needs H.264).

_resolution_note: Capture resolution is per-camera — set cameras.owlsight_left/right width/height/fps independently (Menu > Cameras > Left/Right Camera > Resolution). The mode list merges three sources, each tagged in the menu: 'sensor' = the kernel driver's real mode list (RAW stream, what rpicam-hello --list-cameras prints), 'spec' = a built-in manufacturer table matched via the libcamera id or a dtoverlay= scan of /boot/firmware/config.txt (ov64a40 OwlSight / arducam-64mp Hawkeye / imx519 / imx708 / imx477 / imx378 / imx219 / ov5647 / imx298 / imx415 / imx335 / imx296 / ov9281), 'scaled' = ISP-resized standard sizes. fps is probed per mode on this Pi at startup; datasheet modes the pipeline rejects are dropped. fps is a best-effort target; libcamera clamps to the sensor's achievable rate at the chosen size. A legacy top-level 'resolution' block that forced both eyes equal is still honoured on load for old configs but is retired on the next save.

qr._note: Persisted QR scan state. scan_main=OWLsight cameras, scan_usb=USB cameras.

map._note2: The minimap module is always on (forced by hud_dock); 'enabled' is kept for compatibility.
map._note: map_path is the full path to the last-used map image. pan_x/y and calibration are reset each session.

info_panel._about: Cycling info panel — an opposite-side twin of the minimap (same footprint). Position comes from hud_dock; size mirrors the map. 'show' is [clock, notifications, schedule, weather-now, weather-precip]. clock_face: 0=ticks, 1=numbers, 2=minimal.

hud_dock._about: Docks the minimap (always right) and info panel (always left). bottom: false=top edge, true=bottom edge. v_offset: fine vertical nudge in pixels (Location menu Up/Down).

weather._about: Weather widget (Open-Meteo, no API key; fetched via curl). auto_locate uses IP geolocation; otherwise set lat/lon. metric: true=C/kph, false=F/mph. Needs the device online + curl installed.

splash._image_note: PNG (RGBA preferred). Relative paths resolve from the binary directory; absolute paths used as-is. Example: "assets/branding/logo.png".
splash.title: PROTOHUD
splash.subtitle: XR HUD System
